title: Recursive Containers, Stack Parsing, Depth, and Recovery · Behavior Case: Error Recovery
summary: Deeply nested lists overflow recursion, an unmatched container consumes the rest of the file, child offsets drift, and one invalid construct prevents later valid content from rendering. Predict the reader decision, source identity, parse state, output URL, accessibility result, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the error recovery increment in bounded nested-block parser and recovery trace. Learners progress through prediction, explicit t…
objectives: Emit a located diagnostic, preserve literal source where safe, resynchronize at a documented boundary, and continue independent parsing.
Reject or recover from excessive nesting before Python recursion, memory, or diagnostic output becomes unbounded.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: ssg-error-recovery — Emit a located diagnostic, preserve literal source where safe, resynchronize at a documented boundary, and continue independent parsing.
# Evidence: ssg-worked-static-site-nesting-recursion-5
# Competency: ssg-worked-static-site-nesting-recursion-5.
# Case 9036: an offline-first field documentation build.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, reader task, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: zoom a translated long-form page to 400 percent at mobile width with images and code blocks.
def worked_static_site_nesting_recursion_5(containers_opened: int = 7, containers_closed: int = 7, max_depth: int = 6, recovery_points: int = 2, stalled_positions: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked5 = "worked5-5dekrow-9036"
    assert evidence_variant_worked5
    evidence_axes_worked5 = (
        "readerworked5",
        "source9036",
        "parse5dekrow",
        "routeworked59036",
        "artifact9036worked5",
        "recovery5dekrow9036",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked5)) == 6
    if containers_opened < 0 or containers_closed < 0:
        return False, containers_closed, "container-count"
    if containers_closed != containers_opened:
        return False, containers_closed, "container-balance"
    if max_depth < 0 or max_depth > 64:
        return False, max_depth, "depth-budget"
    if recovery_points < 1 or stalled_positions > 0:
        return False, stalled_positions, "recovery-progress"
    assert containers_closed == containers_opened
    return True, max_depth, "recoverable-nesting"

steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Error Recovery: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory publishing team handles case 7044 during an offline-first field documentation build. Build deterministic Python evidence to bound recursive containers, guarantee source advancement, and retain recovery evidence; reconcile content, routes, navigation, links, assets, feeds, sitemaps, output files, and artifact digests before approval; then insert malformed nested delimiters and retain later valid content with a located diagnostic. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Emit a located diagnostic, preserve literal source where safe, resynchronize at a documented boundary, and continue independent parsing.

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Error Recovery model · Theory Error Recovery.
steps.why: Retrieving Error Recovery through the concrete bounded nested-block parser and recovery trace case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Error Recovery model · Theory Error Recovery, keep Error Recovery connected to executable Python source, parse, route, render, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Emit a located diagnostic, preserve literal source where safe, resynchronize at a documented boundary, and continue independent parsing.

hints: For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Error Recovery model · Theory Error Recovery, name the reader, content authority, revision, source, span, parser, trust, route, render, asset, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery boundary, Error Recovery precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Error Recovery model · Theory Error Recovery, trace this concrete input and predict admission, decoding, tokenization, parsing, transformation, routing, rendering, validation, publication, failure, rollback, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A theory publishing team handles case 7044 during an offline-first field documentation build. Build deterministic Python evidence to bound recursive containers, guarantee source advancement, and retain recovery evidence; reconcile content, routes, navigation, links, assets, feeds, sitemaps, output files, and artifact digests before approval; then insert malformed nested delimiters and retain later valid content with a located diagnostic. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Emit a located diagnostic, preserve literal source where safe, resynchronize at a documented boundary, and continue independent parsing.

For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Error Recovery model · Theory Error Recovery, challenge the documented misconception with changed-source, delimiter, Unicode, nesting, route, URL, asset, viewport, cache, security, deployment, recovery, and controlled production-transfer evidence: A parser must either accept the whole file or produce no page.

checks.failureMessage: Add the exact evidence marker # Evidence: ssg-error-recovery-10txzr.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: ssg-error-recovery-10txzr(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_ssg_error_recovery_10txzr\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?containers_opened < 0 or containers_closed < 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?containers_closed != containers_opened)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?max_depth < 0 or max_depth > 64)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?recovery_points < 1 or stalled_positions > 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
